name: stressed_assets
what: Consolidated stressed/distressed-asset view across three previously-scattered sources: (1) company-level credit-rating distress flags from the layer-32 DB (stressed_assets VIEW), (2) national distressed-LAND supply channels + named liquidation assets (sibling policy-recs repo), (3) the Dunlop India litigation case map (worked single-asset example). Real registers (IBBI/NLMC/SARFAESI) named with routes, not yet ingested.

company_distress_flags.note: materialized as the `stressed_assets` view in companies.db; these are RATING/LITIGATION signals, NOT a formal NPA/IBC classification

pools.scale: 1,436 corporate-debtor liquidation processes ONGOING (assets not yet sold) out of 2,728 that have entered liquidation; 824 of these have been running >2 years. Manufacturing is the single largest sector in insolvency, ~40% of all CIRP admissions, so a large share of these liquidations hold factory land/sheds. Asset-on-ground values are steeply distressed: for the 211 largest liquidation cases, assets were valued at only ~Rs 0.45 trillion against Rs 9.59 trillion of admitted claims.
pools.unit: corporate debtors / processes (each may hold one or more industrial land+building parcels)
pools.period: Ongoing liquidations as on 31-Jan-2025; sector mix and big-case valuation as on 31-Dec-2024
pools.source: IBBI Quarterly Newsletter Jan-Mar 2025 and IBBI CIRP/liquidation data (as on 31-Jan-2025 / 31-Dec-2024); manufacturing 40% share via FACTLY analysis of IBBI data

state_wise.detail: Many liquidation auction notices concentrated in MIDC estates (Thane/Murbad/Nashik) e.g. Vindhyavasini Steel (11,514 sqm Murbad MIDC), General Composites (42,200 sqm Shahapur, Thane), Eurotas Infrastructure plant at Sinnar-Nashik
No official state-wise acreage split of liquidation land is published by IBBI; parcels are spread across all major manufacturing states and listed case-by-case on the auction-notice portal

pools.access_route: IBBI liquidation auction-notice list (ibbi.gov.in/liquidation-auction-notices/lists) linking each liquidator's e-auction PDF; bidding now runs mainly through BAANKNET (ibbi.baanknet.com, the PSB Alliance / ex-eBKray platform) and private auction platforms (auctiontiger.in, bankeauctions.com)
pools.cost_speed_advantage: Land is already zoned industrial with existing plant/shed, roads, power and often environmental clearances; sold under a court-supervised liquidation at fair/reserve value that is typically a fraction of admitted claims (distressed pricing). No fresh land acquisition, no change-of-use, and title passes free of most prior encumbrances via the liquidation sale — far faster than greenfield assembly.

pools.scale: 26 Central PSEs listed as closed or on the verge of closure (a defined universe whose factory land is being freed for monetisation). CPSEs had referred ~3,400 acres of surplus land to DIPAM for potential monetisation (Economic Survey 2021-22). Telecom PSUs alone: BSNL + MTNL have raised Rs 12,984.86 crore from asset monetisation since 2019, of which land & building = ~Rs 2,388 cr (BSNL) + ~Rs 2,135 cr (MTNL) up to Jan-2025.
pools.unit: companies (26 closed CPSEs) / acres (~3,400 acres referred) / Rs crore realised
pools.period: Closed-CPSE list current (2026); ~3,400 acres figure = Economic Survey 2021-22; BSNL/MTNL realisation up to Jan-2025
pools.source: DPE/MSME Sambandh closed-CPSE portal; Economic Survey 2021-22 (via NLMC coverage); Business Standard on BSNL/MTNL monetisation

state_wise.detail: Named closed CPSEs with plant land include Hindustan Cables, Hindustan Fertilizer Corp, Hindustan Paper Corp, HMT Watches, Fertilizer Corp of India, Birds Jute & Exports, Central Inland Water Transport Corp — sites across West Bengal, Bihar, Karnataka, Assam etc.
NLMC valuing ~35,591 sqm (8.7 acres) of BSNL freehold land as a pilot

pools.access_route: National Land Monetisation Corporation (NLMC), nlmc.dpe.gov.in — the SPV set up to hold and monetise surplus/non-core land of closed and strategic-disinvestment CPSEs; also DIPAM asset-monetisation cell
pools.cost_speed_advantage: Government-owned, single-owner land, usually large contiguous industrial parcels with legacy infrastructure; monetised through a structured NLMC/DIPAM process rather than open-market acquisition, avoiding fragmented private title and acquisition delay.

pools.scale: State industrial corporations hold large stocks of allotted-but-unutilised industrial plots they cancel/resume and re-offer. Gujarat (GIDC): ~1,800 hectares (~4,450 acres) of unutilised plots targeted for take-back/re-allotment under the Jan-2024 voluntary-return + resumption policy. Uttar Pradesh (UPSIDA), Maharashtra (MIDC), Rajasthan (RIICO) run parallel cancel-and-reallot programs; exact acreages not centrally published.
pools.period: GIDC policy announced 7-Jan-2024; UPSIDA/RIICO programs active 2024-2026
pools.source: ThePrint on Gujarat GIDC unutilised-plot policy; Deccan Herald / SC ruling on UPSIDA plot cancellations; RIICO Direct Allotment Policy 2025

state_wise.detail: GIDC: ~1,800 ha unutilised across estates; voluntary-return refund up to 75% of current allotment price, plots then re-allotted (portal: egov.gidcgujarat.org)

UPSIDA cancels unutilised plots (unit not built in stipulated time) and re-advertises them; SC on 6-Apr-2026 upheld cancellation of Piaggio's 33-acre Surajpur plot. Availability portal: eservices.onlineupsida.com / onlineupsidc.com Advertisement page
MIDC lists vacant/available plots (incl. resumed) via MILAAP land-allotment portal (land.midcindia.org, services.midcindia.org/vacantplotsdetails)
RIICO Direct Allotment Policy-2025 offers plots at reserve rate; e-auction/lottery + direct allotment via SSO portal

pools.access_route: State industrial-development-corporation portals: GIDC (gidc.gujarat.gov.in), MIDC MILAAP (land.midcindia.org), UPSIDA (eservices.onlineupsida.com), RIICO (riico.rajasthan.gov.in) — each publishes vacant/resumed-plot advertisements and e-auction/allotment notices
pools.cost_speed_advantage: These are inside developed industrial estates with existing roads, power, water and effluent infrastructure; often re-offered at old/subsidised reserve rates or the original allotment price; allotment is administrative (application/e-auction) rather than land acquisition, so possession can be quick.

pools.scale: No consolidated acreage figure is published, but ARCs (ARCIL, government-backed NARCL, etc.) and secured lenders hold and auction large inventories of mortgaged industrial property enforced under the SARFAESI Act, 2002. Qualitative/large pool; the sector is consolidating (Aditya Birla ARC, India Resurgence, Arcion exited).
pools.source: LiveLaw on ARC regulatory landscape; NARCL / ARCIL / bank SARFAESI auction notices

state_wise.detail: SARFAESI industrial-property auctions posted continuously by SBI, PSBs, ARCIL, NARCL and ARC panels; no state-wise land register published

pools.access_route: SARFAESI/ARC e-auction aggregators: auctiontiger.in, bankeauctions.com, BAANKNET (ibbi.baanknet.com), plus individual bank/ARC auction-notice pages (ARCIL, NARCL, Punjab & Sind Bank ARC/NARCL notice pages)
pools.cost_speed_advantage: Enforced mortgaged assets sold at reserve value to recover dues, frequently below replacement cost; already operational/industrial sites. But: unlike IBC, SARFAESI has no statutory resolution timeline and weaker anti-promoter-reacquisition safeguards, so title/possession disputes can slow handover.

pools.scale: NLMC is the dedicated GoI SPV (incorporated 3-Jun-2022, 100% government-owned under DPE/Ministry of Finance) to own, hold, manage and monetise surplus and non-core land of CPSEs under closure and under strategic disinvestment. Pipeline seeded by the ~3,400 acres CPSEs referred to DIPAM (Economic Survey 2021-22); has begun valuation pilots (e.g. 8.7 acres BSNL land in 5 southern cities).
pools.period: Incorporated Jun-2022; pipeline figure ES 2021-22; pilots 2024-2025
pools.source: PIB Cabinet approval note; Economic Survey 2021-22; NLMC official site

state_wise.detail: Covers surplus land of CPSEs such as MTNL, BSNL, BPCL, B&R, BEML, HMT, Instrumentation Ltd — at various stages

pools.cost_speed_advantage: Aggregates government surplus land into a single monetisation vehicle so developers deal with one counterparty holding clean government title, bypassing private-land assembly.
pools.status: operational (corporation live; transaction pipeline still ramping, several assets only at valuation stage — partly proposal/early-execution)

best_access_routes: IBBI liquidation auction-notice list (https://ibbi.gov.in/liquidation-auction-notices/lists) -> individual liquidator e-auction PDFs, with bidding via BAANKNET (https://ibbi.baanknet.com)

State industrial-corporation vacant/resumed-plot portals: GIDC (gidc.gujarat.gov.in / egov.gidcgujarat.org), MIDC MILAAP (land.midcindia.org), UPSIDA (eservices.onlineupsida.com), RIICO (riico.rajasthan.gov.in)
NLMC (nlmc.dpe.gov.in) + DIPAM (dipam.gov.in) for closed/strategic-disinvestment CPSE surplus land
SARFAESI/ARC e-auction aggregators: auctiontiger.in, bankeauctions.com, and NARCL (narcl.co.in) / ARCIL (arcil.co.in) auction notices
caveats: No single official statistic exists for 'total acres of industrial land in IBC liquidation' - IBBI reports counts of processes and rupee values, not aggregate land area; acreage must be built up parcel-by-parcel from auction notices.
The manufacturing ~40% share is of CIRP admissions (going into insolvency), not specifically of liquidations, and not every manufacturing debtor owns land (some lease it).

Gujarat's ~1,800 ha and UPSIDA cancellations include plots never built on (allottee default) as well as genuinely distressed units - this is 'unutilised/resumed' land, broader than only insolvency-linked land.

CIRP (resolution) keeps the company a going concern - land is NOT freely available; the repurposing opportunity is mostly in LIQUIDATION (asset sale) and in resumed/cancelled state plots. This JSON separates them.

IBBI deleted 'sale as a going concern' from the liquidation framework in Oct-2025, which should push more liquidation land toward piecemeal/parcel asset sales (favourable for buyers wanting the land only).

NLMC is operational as a corporation but its transaction pipeline is still early - several CPSE assets are only at the valuation stage, so treat large land-bank figures as pipeline, not sold/available inventory.
SARFAESI/ARC assets can have title/possession disputes and (unlike IBC) no statutory resolution timeline, so handover can be slower and messier than a court-supervised liquidation sale.
Several IBBI auction-notice URLs are per-case PDFs that rotate/expire; the durable entry point is the liquidation-auction-notice list page.

worked_case_dunlop.entity: Dunlop India Ltd. (formerly Dunlop Rubber Co. (India) Ltd., inc. 1929)
worked_case_dunlop.distress: declared sick under SICA 1985; ordered WOUND UP by Calcutta HC 31-Jan-2013 (C.P. 233/2008), appeal dismissed 02-May-2013; Official Liquidator in possession
worked_case_dunlop.key_allegation: ~Rs 2,300 cr of four valuable properties allegedly transferred surreptitiously while before BIFR (Div. Bench order 02-May-2013)

asset_threads.TN_Ambattur: ~60.86 acres assigned land (of ~149 acres acquired 1959); sold Rs 24.34 cr (2004) to V.N. Devadoss via BIFR/AAIFR; GoTN held sale in breach (G.O. Ms. 183, 31-Mar-2008); workers' + Dunlop challenges dismissed (res judicata) — SC Devadoss stamp-duty ruling 08-May-2009
asset_threads.WB_KingsCourt: 50% share in 'Kings Court', 46B Chowringhee Rd, Kolkata (5-storey, 12 flats); sale-notice valuation Rs 29.44 cr (2024); OL sale contested by Salasar Towers (pre-emption) + Eyelid Mercantiles (unregistered 2006 agreement, no title per Suraj Lamp); lead judgment 17-Mar-2026 (J. Raja Basu Chowdhury): auction stands, Salasar may match, Eyelid gets liberty to sue for specific performance

other_properties: Worli, Mumbai (OL sought leave to take over)
worked_case_dunlop.status: live liquidation — Kings Court 50% under OL auction as of the 17-Mar-2026 Calcutta HC judgment (indiankanoon.org/doc/110197805)
worked_case_dunlop.twin_relevance: the worked template for a manufacturer stressed asset: a wound-up factory whose LAND is the residual value, tied up in multi-forum litigation — exactly the IBC/liquidation pool the land dataset counts in aggregate. Ambattur/Sahaganj are the physical parcels; the litigation is the acquisition risk

caveats: The company_distress_flags are RATING-channel signals on companies the twin already tracks — NOT an exhaustive stressed-asset universe.
No single official 'total acres in IBC liquidation' statistic exists (IBBI reports process counts, not acreage) — see land caveats.
Dunlop facts are from a due-diligence workbook; verify the live auction status before acting (17-Mar-2026 judgment is the latest).
next: wire the IBBI liquidation-auction-notice list (ibbi.gov.in/liquidation-auction-notices/lists) as a real machine-readable stressed-asset feed — the one register that publishes structured per-asset notices

register.access: server-rendered table, no auth; ?page=N (20 rows/page, ~481 pages to inception); durable parquet at data/registers/ibbi_liquidation.parquet

state_coverage.note: state is NOT a register field — parsed from the property address in nature_of_assets (~10 pct of rows) + PDF-address for the top-60 by reserve price. ~89 pct remain state_unknown; full coverage needs parsing all 9531 notice PDFs.

state_resolution.friction_method: RULE-BASED proxy (low/medium/high) — NOT a probability of a court outcome, NOT legal advice. Signals: asset liquidity + re-auction/stuck (repeat notices or falling reserve) + ticket size. Lower friction ~= faster, cleaner realisation.
state_resolution.friction_reading: low ~17 pct of notices — single-lot land/flat, fresh notice, small ticket: quickest to clear. high — going-concern/composite, re-auctioned or >Rs100cr: expect multi-forum drag (the Dunlop pattern).
